open_new_part.description: Opens a new part document in SolidWorks, establishing the active document context for subsequent operations.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.template_path: string (optional — full path to a part template; uses the default part template if omitted)

open_document.description: Opens an EXISTING file from disk (counterpart to open_new_part, which makes a blank doc). Native .sldprt/.sldasm/.slddrw open directly by type (from the extension). Foreign formats use a STAGED import (2026-07-09, proven live): OpenDoc6 first; on null (with 3D Interconnect issues OpenDoc6 returns errors=2097152 swFileRequiresRepairError even for valid files) fall back to LoadFile4 = the CLASSIC translator (STEP/IGES/NX .prt proven); as a last resort enable Interconnect (toggle 691) and retry once. as_assembly=true (foreign only): an ASSEMBLY STEP - the classic translator would import it as a MULTIBODY PART, so Interconnect is enabled just for that open (toggle restored after). Makes the opened document active and bumps state_version.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.file_path: string (required — full path to the file to open; .sldprt/.sldasm/.slddrw native, .ipt/.CATPart/STEP/IGES/Parasolid imported as a part via 3D Interconnect)
params.as_assembly: boolean (optional, default false - foreign files only: import an assembly STEP as a real ASSEMBLY via 3D Interconnect instead of the classic multibody-part import; NOTE on this install OpenDoc6-via-Interconnect currently fails with swFileRequiresRepairError - the fallback path for assembly ground truth is per-part import + STEP-parsed placements)

open_document.output: → see execution-response.json — CadState.ActiveDocument = opened doc title; DocumentType = PART/ASSEMBLY/DRAWING; Features[0] = file name

create_sketch.description: Creates a new sketch on a plane OR on an existing model face. Default: plane name. With on_face=true, select the face by face_index (PREFERRED — the i-th face from analyze_model(faces); robust on revolve end-caps where a coordinate pick is ambiguous) or by a point (face_x,face_y,face_z) on the planar face. Enables sketching on existing geometry (e.g. a recess/hub on a part face, a bore on a shaft end). Auto-exits any active sketch first.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.plane: string (plane mode — e.g. Front Plane, Top Plane, Right Plane, or a named ref plane like Plane1; required unless on_face)
params.on_face: boolean (optional, default false — sketch on a model face instead of a plane)
params.face_index: integer (on_face, PREFERRED — index of the target face from analyze_model(faces); bypasses coordinate picking. Takes priority over face_x/y/z)
params.face_x: number (on_face — X of a point ON the target planar face, meters)
params.face_y: number (on_face — Y of a point ON the target planar face, meters)
params.face_z: number (on_face — Z of a point ON the target planar face, meters)

add_sketch_entity.description: Adds a sketch entity to the active sketch. Supported types: 'rectangle' (x1,y1,x2,y2), 'circle' (cx,cy,radius), 'line' (x1,y1,x2,y2), 'arc' (x1,y1 start, x2,y2 end, xm,ym mid-arc point), 'arc_center' (cx,cy exact center, x1,y1 start, x2,y2 end, direction +1 CCW/-1 CW — radius-exact, stable for shallow arcs; created with SketchManager.AddToDB=true so SW input inference/snapping cannot skew the radius), 'ellipse' (cx,cy center, x1,y1 major-axis point, x2,y2 minor-axis point), 'spline' (points=[x1,y1,...] through-points), 'fillet' (vx,vy vertex to round, radius), 'chamfer' (vx,vy vertex to cut, distance). Mirror of analyze_model's sketch-segment output (every emitted segment kind maps to an entity_type here). On COMPLETED the response carries result_geometry: the REAL created geometry read back from SW (not the input) for in-band self-verification. New entity types are added at the execution layer without changing this schema.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.x1: number (rectangle/line/arc/arc_center: X of first/start point; ellipse: X of major-axis point)
params.y1: number (rectangle/line/arc/arc_center: Y of first/start point; ellipse: Y of major-axis point)
params.x2: number (rectangle/line/arc/arc_center: X of second/end point; ellipse: X of minor-axis point)
params.y2: number (rectangle/line/arc/arc_center: Y of second/end point; ellipse: Y of minor-axis point)
params.distance: number (chamfer: equal distance on both sides)
params.direction: number (arc_center: sweep sense from start to end, +1 CCW / -1 CW; default +1; ignored by other types)
params.points: string (spline: JSON array of flat through-points '[x1,y1,x2,y2,...]', >= 2 points; parsed by the adapter into a number array; ignored by other types)
params.construction: boolean (optional, default false — convert the created entity to CONSTRUCTION/reference geometry; mirrors analyze_model's per-segment 'construction' flag so analyzed construction entities can be reproduced)

add_sketch_entity.output: → see execution-response.json (add_sketch_entity additionally returns result_geometry: the real created segment geometry, read-only — does NOT affect state_version)

add_sketch_entities.description: Batch form of add_sketch_entity: creates MANY sketch entities in the active sketch in ONE call. 'segments' is an array of entity records, each record = add_sketch_entity's params for one entity (entity_type + its coordinates; per-record 'construction' supported; a spline record's 'points' is a plain number array). Records are created in array order under ONE AddToDB bracket. NOT transactional: on the first failing record the call returns FAILED naming segments[i] and how many earlier records were created (they remain in the sketch); state_version bumps only on COMPLETED. result_geometry echoes a compact {segment_count, counts per entity_type} — no per-segment geometry (use analyze_model sketch mode for exact readback).

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.segments: string (required — JSON array of entity records '[{"entity_type":"line","x1":0,...},...]'; parsed by the adapter into an object array)

add_sketch_constraint.description: Applies a geometric constraint to sketch entities in the active sketch. Single-entity constraints (horizontal, vertical) need only px1/py1. Two-entity constraints (coincident, parallel, perpendicular, tangent, equal, midpoint) also require px2/py2. entity_type1/2 defaults to 'SKETCHSEGMENT'; use 'SKETCHPOINT' for point-based constraints.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.px1: number (required — X coordinate of point on first entity)
params.py1: number (required — Y coordinate of point on first entity)
params.px2: number (optional — X coordinate of point on second entity; required for two-entity constraints)
params.py2: number (optional — Y coordinate of point on second entity; required for two-entity constraints)

add_dimension.description: Applies a smart dimension to a sketch segment by selecting it via a point on the segment. px/py must lie on or very near the target segment (e.g. the midpoint of a line). This approach works reliably for any sketch geometry, not just simple rectangles.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.px: number (X coordinate of a point on the target segment, in document units)
params.py: number (Y coordinate of a point on the target segment, in document units)
params.label_offset_x: number (optional, default 0.0 — X offset from px for the dimension label position)
params.label_offset_y: number (optional, default -0.015 — Y offset from py for the dimension label position)

extrude_feature.description: Creates a 3D feature from the active sketch profile. Exits sketch mode automatically. feature_type discriminator: 'boss' (solid extrusion, default), 'cut' (material removal, requires existing solid body), 'revolve' (solid of revolution — requires angle and axis coords), 'sweep' (swept solid — requires path_sketch name), 'loft' (lofted solid — requires profiles JSON array of ≥2 sketch names).

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.depth: number (required for boss/cut — extrusion/cut depth in document units)
params.angle: number (revolve only — revolution angle in DEGREES, default 360 = full revolve)
params.axis_x1: number (revolve only — X of first endpoint of centerline segment)
params.axis_y1: number (revolve only — Y of first endpoint of centerline segment)
params.axis_x2: number (revolve only — X of second endpoint of centerline segment)
params.axis_y2: number (revolve only — Y of second endpoint of centerline segment; default 0.001)
params.path_sketch: string (sweep only — name of the sketch containing the sweep path)
params.profiles: string (loft only — JSON array of sketch names e.g. '["Sketch1","Sketch2"]', minimum 2)
params.reverse: boolean (boss/cut — flip feature direction; needed for a cut/boss sketched on a part face where material is on the opposite side)
params.through: boolean (boss/cut — through-all end condition; depth ignored)
params.up_to_face_index: integer (optional, default -1 = off; boss/cut — >= 0 selects the UP-TO-SURFACE end condition terminating exactly on that model face; index from analyze_model(faces), same enumeration as create_sketch face_index; depth ignored. The face is append-selected with Mark=1, the FeatureExtrusion3/FeatureCut4 end-condition reference mark)
params.mid_plane: boolean (optional, default false; boss/cut — swEndCondMidPlane: extrudes SYMMETRICALLY about the sketch plane, depth = TOTAL width; conflicts with through/up_to_face_index)

create_rib.description: Creates a RIB feature from the ACTIVE sketch (consumed, like extrude_feature). The profile is OPEN geometry — typically a single line whose extensions hit existing walls (e.g. the diagonal of an L-bracket); SolidWorks extends and thickens it. Uses IFeatureManager.InsertRib (void-returning — creation is verified from the feature tree; a no-op result is retried with the material direction flipped before failing RIB_FAILED). Draft parameters deliberately not exposed in v1.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.two_sided: boolean (optional, default true — thicken symmetrically on both sides of the sketch; false = single-sided, see reverse_thickness_dir)
params.reverse_thickness_dir: boolean (optional, default false — single-sided only: thicken the opposite side of the sketch normal)
params.reverse_material_dir: boolean (optional, default false — flip which side the rib material fills toward; auto-retry covers a wrong guess)
params.is_norm_to_sketch: boolean (optional, default false — false = extrusion parallel to sketch (classic line rib), true = normal to sketch)

create_rib.output: → see execution-response.json (returns the created rib feature name; result_geometry {volume, faces, edges} after the rib)

add_edge_feature.description: Applies a 3D edge modifier to solid body edges. Operates post-extrusion on the solid, NOT on sketch segments. feature_type='fillet': rounds edge(s) with radius_or_distance as radius. feature_type='chamfer': cuts a chamfer — chamfer_type='distance_angle' (setback + angle, default 45°) or 'distance_distance' (D1 + distance2, directional, chamfer_flip swaps sides). PREFERRED edge selection: edge_indices (JSON array of integer indices from analyze_model(edges)) — robust for crowded/concave (inner-corner, small-radius) edges that coordinate picks can't disambiguate. Fallback: ex/ey/ez (single) or edges_json (multiple {ex,ey,ez}).

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.radius_or_distance: number (required — fillet radius, or chamfer first-face setback D1, in meters)
params.edge_indices: string (optional, PREFERRED — JSON array of integer edge indices from analyze_model(edges), e.g. '[3,5]'; bypasses coordinate picking. Takes priority over edges_json/ex-ey-ez)
params.ex: number (single-edge: X coordinate of point on target edge)
params.ey: number (single-edge: Y coordinate of point on target edge)
params.ez: number (single-edge: Z coordinate of point on target edge)
params.edges_json: string (optional — JSON array of {ex,ey,ez} objects for multiple edges, overrides single ex/ey/ez if non-empty)
params.angle: number (chamfer distance_angle only: chamfer angle in DEGREES, default 45)
params.distance2: number (chamfer distance_distance only: second-face setback D2 in meters, required > 0 there)
params.chamfer_flip: boolean (chamfer only: swap which face gets D1 vs D2 / flip direction, default false)

create_drawing.description: Opens a new SolidWorks drawing document (A3 sheet, 1:1 scale). model_path is optional — an empty drawing is created if omitted. add_drawing_view requires the referenced part to be saved to disk.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.model_path: string (optional — full path to the part/assembly to reference; empty drawing created if omitted)

add_drawing_view.description: Adds a model projection view to the active drawing sheet. The referenced part must be saved to disk. view_type maps to SolidWorks standard views.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.pos_x: number (optional, default 0.1 — X position on drawing sheet in meters)
params.pos_y: number (optional, default 0.1 — Y position on drawing sheet in meters)
params.model_path: string (optional — full path to part file; auto-detects first open part if omitted)
params.display_mode: string (optional — 'hlv'|'hlr'|'wireframe'|'shaded'|'shaded_edges'|'default'; '' = drafting default: ortho views→HLV, isometric→document default. IView.SetDisplayMode3, swDisplayMode_e)

add_flat_pattern_view.description: Adds a FLAT PATTERN drawing view of a sheet-metal part (the unfolded blank with bend lines/notes) — the correct, standard way to detail sheet metal (sheet metal is dimensioned on its flat pattern, not the folded views). Uses IDrawingDoc.CreateFlatPatternViewFromModelView3(ModelName, ConfigName, LocX, LocY, LocZ, HideBendLines, FlipView). The part must be sheet metal (have a Flat-Pattern feature) and saved to disk. config_name defaults to the part's active configuration (falls back to 'Default'). Then auto_dimension_drawing places the blank/hole dimensions on it. Bumps state_version; result_geometry echoes {view_name, config, hide_bend_lines}.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.pos_x: number (optional, default 0.1 — X position on drawing sheet in meters)
params.pos_y: number (optional, default 0.1 — Y position on drawing sheet in meters)
params.model_path: string (optional — full path to the sheet-metal part; auto-detects first open part if omitted)
params.config_name: string (optional — configuration to flatten; defaults to the part's active configuration, else 'Default')
params.hide_bend_lines: boolean (optional, default false — hide the bend lines in the flat pattern)
params.flip_view: boolean (optional, default false — flip the flat pattern view)

add_drawing_dimension.description: Applies a smart dimension to a drawing view segment nearest to point (px, py). Same coordinate selection approach as add_dimension but operates in a drawing document. Requires active drawing document.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.px: number (X coordinate of a point on the target drawing segment, in sheet meters)
params.py: number (Y coordinate of a point on the target drawing segment, in sheet meters)

auto_dimension_drawing.description: Automatically transfers the MODEL's driving dimensions into the active drawing's views (SolidWorks 'Insert Model Items > Dimensions'). The robust alternative to add_drawing_dimension's fragile coordinate pick: dimensions come straight from the model's parametric dimensions, correctly placed. Uses IDrawingDoc.InsertModelAnnotations3 (Types = swInsertDimensionsMarkedForDrawing, optionally + NotMarkedForDrawing). Bumps state_version; result_geometry echoes inserted_count. Requires an active drawing document with at least one model view.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.all_views: boolean (optional, default true — insert into all views; false = only the currently selected view)
params.include_unmarked: boolean (optional, default false — also insert driving dims NOT marked for drawing, i.e. ALL driving dims; more complete but noisier. Use if marked-only inserts 0 dims)
params.eliminate_duplicates: boolean (optional, default true — avoid inserting the same model dimension into more than one view)

auto_dimension_drawing.output: → see execution-response.json — result_geometry holds {inserted_count, all_views, include_unmarked}. inserted_count = number of dimensions placed (0 is valid; re-try with include_unmarked=true).

auto_center_marks.description: Automatically places center marks (with extended centerlines) on every hole/slot in every model view of the active drawing (SolidWorks 'Auto Insert > Center Marks', IView.AutoInsertCenterMarks2). Robust and automatic — no coordinate picking (the drawing-side analogue of index-based selection). The difficulty-2 capability a bracket/flange with holes needs on top of auto_dimension_drawing. Bumps state_version; result_geometry echoes the total center-mark count. Requires an active drawing with model views.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.include_slots: boolean (optional, default true — also add slot center marks/centerlines for linear & arc slots, not just round holes)
params.extended_lines: boolean (optional, default true — draw the extended centerlines through each hole, not just a small cross)

auto_center_marks.output: → see execution-response.json — result_geometry holds {center_marks, views_processed, include_slots}. center_marks = total center marks across all model views (0 if the part has no holes/slots).

add_hole_callout.description: Inserts a hole callout (e.g. 'n× ØD THRU') on the hole edge nearest the sheet point (px, py) in the active drawing, via IDrawingDoc.AddHoleCallout2. Coordinate-based — same fragile point pick as add_drawing_dimension (KNOWN-LIMITATIONS #6); prefer auto_center_marks for centerlines and use this for explicit per-hole callouts. Bumps state_version. Requires an active drawing.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.px: number (X coordinate of a point on the target hole's projected circular edge, in sheet meters)
params.py: number (Y coordinate of a point on the target hole's projected circular edge, in sheet meters)

add_section_view.description: Creates a SECTION VIEW to expose internal/blind features (e.g. a blind pocket's depth) as visible, dimensionable edges — the difficulty-3 capability. Two ways to define the cut: EDGE mode (edge_x,edge_y → cut ALONG an existing projected edge, best when a real edge lies on the desired plane) or LINE mode (x1,y1,x2,y2 → draw a cut line, best for cutting THROUGH a feature's interior such as a pocket middle where no edge exists). IDrawingDoc.CreateSectionViewAt5 (with a CreateSectionViewAt2 fallback) places the section at (px,py). Bumps state_version. NOTE: coordinate-based (#6); after it lands, re-run auto_dimension_drawing or add_drawing_dimension to dimension the now-visible depth. Repeated failed attempts can wedge section creation — restart on a clean drawing state.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.edge_x: number (EDGE mode: X of a point on an existing straight projected edge to cut along, sheet meters)
params.edge_y: number (EDGE mode: Y of that point, sheet meters)
params.x1: number (LINE mode: cut line start X, sheet meters)
params.y1: number (LINE mode: cut line start Y, sheet meters)
params.x2: number (LINE mode: cut line end X, sheet meters)
params.y2: number (LINE mode: cut line end Y, sheet meters)
params.px: number (section-view placement X, sheet meters — which side the section projects to)
params.label: string (optional, default 'A' — section label, e.g. 'A' → 'SECTION A-A')
params.flip: boolean (optional, default false — reverse the cut/viewing direction)
params.scale: number (optional — section view scale; inherits the parent's if omitted)

verify_state.description: Reads and returns the current CAD state without modifying the document, used to confirm expected state before or after operations.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

ensure_ready.description: Lifecycle/bootstrap — NOT a state-versioned CAD op. Served on its own POST /ensure_ready route (not /api/tool/execute), so it carries no operation_id/state_version. Ensures the execution server is running and SolidWorks is attached, launching SolidWorks via COM if it is closed. Does NOT open or create any document. Does NOT increment state_version.
ensure_ready.output: { status, comAttached, swLaunched, activeDocument, swVersion, stateVersion } (or comAttached=false + launchError/ensureError on failure)

export_document.description: Exports the active SolidWorks document to a file without closing it. DWG/DXF require an active drawing document. PDF uses SolidWorks PDF export options. state_version is NOT incremented (no CAD state change).

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.format: string (required: 'STEP', 'IGES', 'STL', 'PDF', 'DWG', 'DXF' — DWG/DXF require active drawing doc)
params.file_path: string (required — full output path including filename and extension)

batch_export.description: Exports the active document to multiple formats in one call. Each format saved as file_path_base.<ext>. DWG/DXF skipped if active doc is not a drawing. Partial success reported in response Features list. state_version is NOT incremented.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.file_path_base: string (required — output path without extension, e.g. 'C:/output/mypart')
params.formats_json: string (required — JSON array of format strings, e.g. '["STEP","STL","PDF"]')

close_document.description: Closes the active SolidWorks document, optionally saving before closing.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.save: boolean (true to save before closing, false to discard changes)
params.close_all: boolean (optional, default false - close ALL open documents incl. invisibly-loaded component docs via CloseAllDocuments(true); a per-active-doc close loop can never reach those, and a lingering same-TITLE doc blocks opening another folder's part (error 65536) and locks files

save_document.description: Saves the active SolidWorks document to disk. With file_path, does a SaveAs to that path (creating the directory); without it, an in-place Save (only if the document already has a path). Does NOT increment state_version (no geometry change). A part must be saved to disk before a drawing view can reference it.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.file_path: string (optional — full output path incl. extension: .sldprt/.sldasm/.slddrw; in-place save if omitted)

analyze_model.description: Analyzes the active part document without modifying it. Does NOT increment state_version. analysis_type='mass_properties': returns volume, surface_area, center of gravity (cx/cy/cz). analysis_type='geometry': returns bodies, faces, edges, vertices counts. analysis_type='edges': returns one JSON object listing every solid edge with start/end/midpoint 3D coords (meters, rounded to 6 decimals; closed/circular edges also include length) — pick an edge index (the `i` field) for add_edge_feature edge_indices, or a midpoint for coordinate selection. Slowest mode on very large parts (COM-bound). analysis_type='faces': returns one JSON object listing every solid face with a stable index `i` (planar faces also carry normal, area, and a representative on-plane point) — pick a face index for create_sketch(on_face, face_index). analysis_type='features': the COMPACT part recipe (default 'understand the part' read) — ordered feature tree (name/type, suppressed only when true), deduped driving dimensions (SI, rounded to 6 decimals), a per-sketch SUMMARY (segment counts + an inline {cx,cy,r} for single-full-circle profiles; NO per-segment coordinate dump), each sketch's plane {ref:'Front/Top/Right Plane', offset}, each extrude/cut's {end, depth (blind only), reversed}, pattern semantics, and equations + globals; features are in tree (history) order. analysis_type='sketch' (requires 'name'): ONE sketch's full geometry (every segment, rounded to 6 decimals) + plane — use only when exact sketch coordinates are needed. analysis_type='feature_map': per-feature geometry ATTRIBUTION — walks the tree base→end with the ROLLBACK BAR (swMoveRollbackBarTo_e) and diffs the topology between stops inside the tool; consumed_edges (both endpoints gone — trimmed neighbours excluded) are the deterministic fillet/chamfer edge anchors (PRE-feature geometry), created_faces are genuinely new surfaces (trimmed existing planes excluded). Non-destructive: the bar is restored to the end in finally; nothing is saved.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.name: string (required only for analysis_type='sketch' — the sketch feature name, e.g. 'Sketch2')
params.from_feature: string (optional, feature_map only — start the walk at this tree feature name; default = tree base)
params.to_feature: string (optional, feature_map only — end the walk at this tree feature name; default = tree end)
params.near: string (optional, edges/faces only — JSON '[x,y,z]' in meters; return only entities nearest this point instead of the full dump, each annotated with dist and its stable index i; blank = full dump, backward compatible)
params.k: integer (optional, edges/faces only — keep only the k nearest to 'near'; 0 = no cap)
params.axis: string (optional, edges/faces only — JSON '[x,y,z]'; keep only entities whose direction is ~parallel to axis: faces filter by planar NORMAL, edges by chord direction)

analyze_model.output: → see execution-response.json — results encoded in CadState.Features list. mass_properties/geometry: flat 'key=value' strings. edges: a single JSON string {edge_count, edges:[{i,start,end,mid}]} (closed/circular edges also carry length). faces: a single JSON string {face_count, faces:[{i, planar, area, normal?, point?}]} (normal/point only for planar faces). features: a single consolidated JSON string {feature_count, features[], equations[]} — sketches carry a summary (counts [+circle]); NO raw segments. sketch: a single JSON string {name, sketch:{counts, segments[]}, plane} for the one named sketch. feature_map: a single JSON string {feature_count, map:[{feature, type, suppressed?, delta:{faces,edges,vertices}, consumed_edges:[{mid,len}], created_faces:[{point, normal?, planar, area}]}]} — coords in meters rounded to 6 decimals; delta/consumed/created present only for unsuppressed solid-affecting features (sketches/ref geometry are listed name+type only).

analyze_drawing.description: Analyzes the active DRAWING document without modifying it (read-only, does NOT increment state_version) — the drawing-side sibling of analyze_model. Walks the views (IDrawingDoc.GetFirstView/GetNextView; the first view is the SHEET) and reports each view's name, type (swDrawingViewTypes_e int), scale, sheet position, and its display dimensions (full-name + SI value). Feeds the objective Stage-1 check (do a produced drawing's dims match the model?) and the Stage-2 re-modeling input.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.include_geometry: boolean (optional, default false — also return each view's projected 2D geometry as clean primitives geometry:{lines, curves, frame} via IView.GetPolylines7, for reverse-engineering a part from its drawing independent of dimension clutter; coords in model-scale meters centered on the view, z=0; line segments carry the up/down / which-face profile structure a dim value can't)

analyze_drawing.output: → see execution-response.json — CadState.Features holds a single JSON string {view_count, dimension_count, views:[{name, type, scale, pos:[x,y], dimensions:[{name, value_si}], geometry?:{lines:[{x1,y1,x2,y2}], curves:[{n,x1,y1,xm,ym,x2,y2}], frame}}]}. view[0] is the drawing sheet. value_si in meters/radians, rounded to 6 decimals. geometry present only when include_geometry=true.

get_selection.description: Reports what the USER currently has selected in the SolidWorks GUI — the inverse of index-based selection. When the user clicks a face/edge/vertex/plane in the window (e.g. while describing what to do), this returns each selected entity mapped to the SAME stable index analyze_model(faces/edges) uses, so the AI knows exactly which geometry the user means. A selected FACE → {type:'face', i, planar, normal?, area, point?} → feed i to create_sketch(on_face=true, face_index=i). A selected EDGE → {type:'edge', i, start, end, mid} → feed i to add_edge_feature(edge_indices='[i]'). Also reports vertices (point) and reference planes (name). Read-only: does NOT change state_version and does NOT clear the selection — but call it BEFORE any tool that clears the selection (create_sketch/add_edge_feature/extrude cut, etc.).

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

get_selection.output: → see execution-response.json — results encoded in CadState.Features as a single JSON string {selected_count, selection:[{type, i?, ...geometry}]}. type ∈ face|edge|vertex|plane|other. face carries i/planar/area/normal?/point?; edge carries i/start/end/mid(+length if closed); vertex carries point; plane carries name. i = -1 if the selected face/edge could not be matched to a solid-body index.

edit_sketch.description: Reopens an existing sketch feature for editing. Counterpart to create_sketch. Returns COMPLETED with ActiveSketch set to the sketch name. Exit by calling extrude_feature or any feature tool (which exits sketch mode automatically).

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.sketch_name: string (required — exact sketch feature name, e.g. 'Sketch1')

add_reference_geometry.description: Creates reference geometry in the active part. type='plane': offset plane from ref_plane_name by offset (meters). type='axis': axis at intersection of two planes/edges (entity1_name/type and entity2_name/type). type='point': reference point at a vertex (px, py, pz).

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.ref_plane_name: string (plane only — base plane name, e.g. 'Front Plane')
params.offset: number (plane only — offset distance in meters)
params.entity1_name: string (axis only — first plane or edge name)
params.entity2_name: string (axis only — second plane or edge name)
params.px: number (point only — X coordinate of vertex)
params.py: number (point only — Y coordinate of vertex)
params.pz: number (point only — Z coordinate of vertex)

add_reference_geometry.output: → see execution-response.json — created feature name in CadState.Features[0] (e.g. 'Plane1', 'Axis1')

create_pattern.description: Creates a linear, circular, or mirror feature pattern. pattern_type='linear': repeats feature along X/Y/Z direction. pattern_type='circular': repeats feature around a named axis. pattern_type='mirror': mirrors one or more FEATURES about a plane (InsertMirrorFeature2; selection marks features=1, plane=2 per the local CHM). Seed feature must be a solid feature (e.g. 'Boss-Extrude1'). Circular pattern requires a reference axis created with add_reference_geometry.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.feature_name: string (linear/circular: required seed feature name, e.g. 'Boss-Extrude1'; mirror: single-feature shorthand for features_json)
params.spacing: number (linear only — spacing between instances in meters, default 0.01)
params.count: integer (number of instances including seed, default 2)
params.flip: boolean (linear only, default false — pattern toward the NEGATIVE axis; FeatureLinearPattern3's FlipDir1, exposed v0.7.0)
params.count2: integer (linear only — accepted but NON-FUNCTIONAL: no second-direction entity is wired to FeatureLinearPattern3 (recorded gap, 2026-07-16); compose a grid as a pattern OF a pattern instead. Default 1)
params.spacing2: number (linear only — see count2: non-functional until D2 is wired, default 0.01)
params.axis_name: string (circular only — reference axis name, e.g. 'Axis1')
params.angle: number (circular only — degrees; with equal_spacing=true this is the TOTAL spread (use 360 for a full ring), with equal_spacing=false it is the angle BETWEEN adjacent instances)
params.equal_spacing: boolean (circular only, default true — true: angle=total spread, evenly divided; false: angle=per-instance spacing, may wrap/overlap)
params.features_json: string (mirror only — JSON array of feature tree names to mirror, e.g. '["Edge-Flange1","Sketched Bend2"]'; ADR-022 JSON-string idiom)
params.plane: string (mirror only — mirror plane name; canonical English default-plane names resolve on any localization; default 'Right Plane')
params.geometry_pattern: boolean (mirror only, default false — true mirrors geometry without solving each feature)

create_pattern.output: → see execution-response.json — created pattern feature name in CadState.Features[0]

set_part_material.description: Assigns a material to the active part document (all configurations). Applied material appears in Mass Properties and the feature tree. Requires a part document — not a drawing.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.material_name: string (required — EXACT library name, e.g. '1060 Alloy' (NOT 'Aluminum 1060 Alloy'), 'AISI 1020', 'ABS', 'Nylon 6/10')
params.library: string (optional, default 'SolidWorks Materials' — material database name)

set_part_material.output: → see execution-response.json — applied material and library in CadState.Features

sheet_metal_feature.description: Creates sheet metal features on the active part. feature_type='base_flange': converts active sketch profile to sheet metal base (exits sketch automatically). feature_type='edge_flange': adds a default-profile flange to an edge — select it by edge_index (preferred) or a point (ex,ey,ez). feature_type='sketched_bend': bends the sheet about the bend line(s) in the ACTIVE sketch (IFeatureManager.InsertSheetMetal3dBend, CHM-verified; the pre-selected FIXED face stays put — pick it by fixed_face_index (analyze faces numbering, preferred) or fixed_x/y/z; a sketch with multiple bend lines yields one feature with one bend per line). feature_type='flat_pattern': unfolds all bends (requires existing base_flange).

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.thickness: number (base_flange — sheet thickness in meters, default 0.001)
params.bend_radius: number (base_flange/sketched_bend — bend radius in meters, default 0.001; sketched_bend ignores it when use_default_radius=true)
params.k_factor: number (base_flange — K-factor for bend allowance, default 0.5)
params.ex: number (edge_flange — X coordinate of point on target edge)
params.ey: number (edge_flange — Y coordinate of point on target edge)
params.ez: number (edge_flange — Z coordinate of point on target edge)
params.flange_length: number (edge_flange — flange length in meters, default 0.02)
params.angle: number (edge_flange/sketched_bend — bend angle in degrees, default 90.0)
params.use_default_radius: boolean (sketched_bend only, default false — true uses the sheet's default bend radius)
params.flip: boolean (sketched_bend only, default false — reverse the bend direction)
params.bend_position: string (sketched_bend only — 'centerline' (default), 'material_inside', 'material_outside', or 'bend_outside'; matches the `position` analyze_model(features) reports on an SM3dBend)
params.fixed_face_index: integer (sketched_bend, optional — the FIXED face's index from analyze_model(faces); preferred over fixed_x/y/z)
params.fixed_x: number (sketched_bend — X of a 3D point ON the fixed face, meters)
params.fixed_y: number (sketched_bend — Y of a 3D point ON the fixed face, meters)
params.fixed_z: number (sketched_bend — Z of a 3D point ON the fixed face, meters)
params.reverse_thickness: boolean (base_flange only, default false — thicken to the OPPOSITE side of the sketch plane; derive from which side the original blank's faces sit, e.g. feature_map's SMBaseFlange created faces)
params.symmetric_thickness: boolean (base_flange only, default false — thicken BOTH ways off the sketch plane (±t/2, mid-plane style); reproduce the original's own flag — it also sets the intrinsic sheet orientation downstream bend directions fold against)
params.clear_profile: boolean (edge_flange_sketch only, default true — wipe the generated default rectangle so the caller redraws the ORIGINAL's custom profile via add_sketch_entity; the op leaves the generated sketch ACTIVE and echoes its MEASURED frame in result_geometry, then edge_flange_finish creates the flange from it — SW's documented custom-profile flow; the flange API accepts only a sketch IT generated)
params.edge_index: integer (edge_flange/edge_flange_sketch/edge_flange_finish, optional — the attach edge's stable index from analyze_model(edges); PREFERRED over ex/ey/ez, whose coordinate pick can miss a real edge (KNOWN-LIMITATIONS #6). Since v0.7.0 the simple edge_flange accepts it too (shared SelectFlangeEdge). For the two-call custom-profile flow pass the SAME index to both calls — the sketch step adds no solid geometry, so edge enumeration is stable)

modify_dimension.description: Universal parametric edit: sets a named display dimension's value in SI units (meters for length, radians for angle), rebuilds, and bumps state_version. Pairs with analyze_model(features), which reports each feature's dimension full-names + SI values. The keystone for building variants.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.name: string (required — dimension full-name exactly as analyze_model(features) reports it, e.g. 'D1@Boss-Extrude1@Part.Part')
params.value: number (required — new value in SI: meters for length/distance, radians for angle)

modify_dimension.output: → see execution-response.json (additionally returns result_geometry {dimension, requested, effective}: the value read back from SolidWorks after rebuild, for in-band verification)

edit_feature.description: Discriminated structural edit of a named feature: suppress | unsuppress | delete | rename. Each rebuilds and bumps state_version. NOTE: suppress/delete change topology, so earlier coordinate/edge/face selections may no longer resolve — re-query analyze_model afterwards.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.new_name: string (required only for action='rename' — the new feature name)

edit_feature.output: → see execution-response.json — CadState.Features[0] holds the (possibly renamed) feature name (delete works for any feature type incl. sketches); empty for delete

activate_document.description: Switches the active SolidWorks document to an already-open one by title. Lifecycle op — does NOT check or increment state_version and does NOT change geometry. Lets a caller read/compare another open document (e.g. activate the original, analyze it, activate the copy).

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.title: string (required — the title of an already-OPEN document, e.g. 'gear' or 'gear.SLDPRT')

activate_document.output: → see execution-response.json — CadState.ActiveDocument holds the now-active document title

get_recipe.description: [ADAPTER-ONLY — no COM, no REST, no state_version] Serves the IR-generation recipe (cad-planner/recipe-usage.md) to the model section-by-section, plus the two cad-planner contract JSONs (feature-graph.schema.json = the capability registry; analysis-artifact.schema.json). The model calls this BEFORE writing an ir.graph or producing a reconstruction-grade drawing — token cost stays proportional to the task (index → only the needed sections). Read-only file serving from the repo; no SolidWorks interaction.

input.operation_id: n/a — pure file read, no execution call
input.state_version: n/a — pure file read, no execution call

params.section: string enum (default 'index' — TOC + version header): 'contract' | 'canonicalization' | 'forward' (intent→IR authoring for submit_feature_graph) | 'mapping' | 'mapping_part' | 'mapping_sheet_metal' | 'mapping_assembly' | 'verification' | 'reverse' (drawing→part reading discipline) | 'coverage' | 'drawing' | 'feature_graph_schema' (IR schema JSON) | 'analysis_artifact_schema' (artifact contract JSON)

get_recipe.output: The requested recipe section as markdown (or the raw contract JSON for the two schema sections).

save_analysis.description: [ADAPTER-ONLY — Phase A analysis pipeline, ADR-040] NOT a COM/execution tool: it has NO ToolController/SolidWorksService case. The adapter orchestrates EXISTING reads (open_document/activate_document + analyze_model features/mass_properties/geometry), computes the file's sha256, and writes the persistent analysis artifact <folder>/.solidpilot/<filename>.analysis.json per cad-planner/contracts/analysis-artifact.schema.json. Artifacts are a CACHE, never truth: identity.source_hash mismatch against the current file = stale, re-analyze. The ir block is left null (the AI/IR pass fills it later — the model gets the mapping rules from the get_recipe tool). Leaves the part open and active.

input.operation_id: n/a at this level — each underlying read carries its own operation_id (uuid4) on /api/tool/execute
input.state_version: n/a at this level — the underlying calls thread the adapter's tracked state_version (one-shot resync-retry on INVALID_STATE_VERSION)

params.file_path: string (required — absolute path of the .SLDPRT to analyze; v0 is parts-only, drawing/assembly artifacts arrive with later pipeline steps)

save_analysis.output: Token-frugal summary string: artifact path + feature/parameter counts + topology counts + linked-drawing count + hash prefix. The artifact JSON itself stays on disk — read it from there when the full content is needed.

rebuild_from_ir.description: ADAPTER-ONLY orchestration (no ToolController/SolidWorksService case) — the analysis pipeline's IR door (IR-ADR-005: two doors, ONE pycompiler). Reads an analysis artifact JSON, takes ir.graph, optionally opens a fresh part, and executes the graph through the SAME deterministic pycompiler as the forward door submit_feature_graph (two doors, one compiler), over the existing /api/tool/execute + /state endpoints. Reports a stale source hash informatively (cache discipline, ADR-040); resyncs the adapter's state_version after the run (many state-bumping sub-ops happen outside the normal _call path).

input.operation_id: n/a at this level — each lowered sub-op carries its own operation_id (uuid4) and state_version on /api/tool/execute
input.state_version: n/a at this level — the adapter resyncs its local state_version from GET /state after the run

params.artifact_path: string (required — absolute path of the <file>.analysis.json artifact whose ir.graph to rebuild)
params.fresh_document: boolean (optional, default true — open a NEW blank part before the rebuild; the normal round-trip flow)

rebuild_from_ir.output: The compiler's per-node summary string: COMPLETED n/n nodes (+ artifact name, + source_stale flag when the source file changed since analysis) or FAILED with the feature-level error and how far it got (partial geometry may remain — CAD ops are not transactional).

submit_feature_graph.description: ADAPTER-ONLY orchestration (no ToolController/SolidWorksService case) — the FORWARD Feature Graph IR door (re-enabled gate-free 2026-07-16, IR-ADR-017): builds a part or assembly from a CAD-neutral Feature Graph IR in ONE call. The adapter's deterministic pycompiler (solidworks-compiler/pycompiler — the SAME compiler rebuild_from_ir uses; two doors, one compiler, IR-ADR-005) lowers each IR node to ordered calls on the EXISTING low-level tools over /api/tool/execute + /state, and resolves references (geometric anchors, runtime feature names) against live geometry. Vocabulary = cad-planner/contracts/feature-graph.schema.json 0.7.0-draft (the capability registry; served to the model via get_recipe). fresh_document=true opens a new blank document first — the graph's node types decide part vs assembly. Resyncs the adapter's state_version after every run (many state-bumping sub-ops happen outside the normal _call path).

input.operation_id: n/a at this level — each lowered sub-op carries its own operation_id (uuid4) and state_version on /api/tool/execute
input.state_version: n/a at this level — the adapter resyncs its local state_version from GET /state after the run

params.graph: string (required — JSON Feature Graph IR per cad-planner/contracts/feature-graph.schema.json; lengths in METERS, angles in RADIANS)
params.fresh_document: boolean (optional, default true — open a new blank document first: part graphs a part, assembly graphs an assembly; false = build into the current active document)

submit_feature_graph.output: Per-node report string: COMPLETED (nodes built) or FAILED (feature-level error + how far it got). Partial geometry may remain on failure (CAD ops are not transactional). Not itself in the state_version/idempotency envelope; its sub-ops are.

compare_parts.description: ADAPTER-ONLY orchestration (no ToolController/SolidWorksService case) — the objective two-part diff / round-trip verifier (ADR-040, A0). Each doc argument is either an absolute .SLDPRT path (opened from disk) or an open document's TITLE (e.g. an unsaved rebuild). Activates each in turn and runs analyze_model(geometry + mass_properties); compares topology (bodies/faces/edges/vertices), volume, surface area, CG. Reports the DECIDED verified-criteria verdict (topology EXACT and |dV| <= 1% and |dA| <= 1%) — writing ir.verification into an artifact stays the caller's job. Geometry-read-only; doc_b is left active. bbox comparison pending (analyze does not expose it).

input.operation_id: n/a at this level — each underlying read carries its own operation_id (uuid4) on /api/tool/execute
input.state_version: n/a at this level — the underlying calls thread the adapter's tracked state_version (one-shot resync-retry on INVALID_STATE_VERSION)

params.doc_a: string (required — REFERENCE document: absolute .SLDPRT path or open-doc title; deltas are relative to it — normally the ORIGINAL)
params.doc_b: string (required — comparison document: absolute .SLDPRT path or open-doc title — normally the REBUILD)
params.detail: string (optional — 'faces' also lists EXTRA (in doc_b only) / MISSING (in doc_a only) surfaces, matched by supporting plane or cylinder axis+radius; blank = summary only)

compare_parts.output: One-line report: verified_criteria PASS/FAIL | topology A/B (EXACT|DIFFER) | volume A/B + dV% | area A/B + dA% | cg_distance. With detail='faces', a trailing faces: matched/MISSING/EXTRA breakdown.

open_new_assembly.description: Opens a new blank ASSEMBLY document (the twin of open_new_part). Template: explicit template_path, else the user-configured default assembly template, else the first *.asmdot found. Bumps state_version.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.template_path: string (optional - full path to an .asmdot template)

open_new_assembly.output: -> see execution-response.json - CadState.ActiveDocument = new doc title; DocumentType = ASSEMBLY

analyze_assembly.description: READ-ONLY assembly analysis (B1, ADR-047) - does NOT change state. analysis_type='components': top-level component occurrences in FEATURE-TREE order (GetComponents is arbitrary - proven live), each with name (instance-numbered Name2), source path, config, fixed/suppressed, children count (subassembly = out-of-scope hit), and the FULL Transform2 (13 numbers: 3x3 rotation row-major + translation + scale, 9dp - the verification ground truth). 'mates': the MateGroup's subfeatures in CREATION order - type/alignment from the ENUMS (never localized display names), SI value for distance/angle from the mate's own dimension, and per entity: owning component, kind (swMateEntity2ReferenceType_e name), EntityParams (ASSEMBLY-space location+direction+radii). 'faces'/'edges' (requires 'component'): that component's entity list with stable indices for add_mate's INDEX-first selection - coordinates are COMPONENT-LOCAL (proven live; the payload carries the component transform for mapping).

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.analysis_type: string (required): 'components' | 'components_flat' (leaf occurrences across nesting levels, root-space transforms — wrapper flattening) | 'mates' | 'faces' | 'edges'
params.component: string (required for faces/edges - the component's Name2, e.g. 'Shaft-1')

analyze_assembly.output: -> see execution-response.json - CadState.Features[0] = one consolidated JSON string

insert_component.description: Insert a component occurrence into the ACTIVE assembly (AddComponent5). Preloads the part document silently, re-activates the assembly (deterministically - CloseDoc leaves the active doc unpredictable, proven live), inserts at x/y/z, optionally applies a FULL 13-number transform (transform_json - needed for arbitrary rotations), and makes the fixed state explicit either way. Response Features[0] = the PLAIN instance-numbered component Name2 (the pycompiler records it as the node's runtime name); also fixed=... and transform=[...] readbacks. Bumps state_version.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.file_path: string (required - the part file to insert; must exist)
params.fixed: boolean (optional - when present the occurrence is explicitly fixed/unfixed)
params.transform_json: string (optional - JSON array of 13 numbers: 3x3 rotation row-major + translation xyz (meters) + scale; applied via IMathUtility.CreateTransform after insert)

add_mate.description: Add a mate between two entities in the ACTIVE assembly (AddMate5). Entity selection is INDEX-FIRST (KNOWN-LIMITATIONS #6): each side = (component Name2, kind face|edge, index from analyze_assembly(faces|edges, component)); the entity object is selected directly (IEntity.Select4, mark 1) - no coordinate/name picks. value: METERS for distance, DEGREES for angle (C4 tool boundary). After the mate: EditRebuild3 + response carries the mate feature name and BOTH sides' component transforms (the per-mate stop check). ErrorStatus 1 = success (swAddMateError_e, observed live). Bumps state_version.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.value: number (optional - METERS for distance, DEGREES for angle)

compare_assemblies.description: ADAPTER-ONLY orchestration (no COM case; composes analyze_assembly + analyze_model reads) - the assembly round-trip verifier behind the artifact verified label (ADR-047 B4, criteria RATIFIED): component set EXACT (source basename + config + instance counts, paired by tree-order ordinal) AND every paired component transform within tolerance (position <= 1um, rotation <= 1e-6) AND mate count + type multiset match AND assembly mass properties within the V1 thresholds (|dV| <= 1%, |dA| <= 1%). doc args = absolute .SLDASM path OR open-document title (unsaved rebuilds compare too). The verdict is a REPORT - writing ir.verification stays the caller's job.

input.operation_id: n/a at this level - each underlying read carries its own
input.state_version: n/a at this level - reads are non-bumping; open/activate bumps flow through _call_raw

params.doc_a: string (required - the REFERENCE assembly: path or open title; normally the original)
params.doc_b: string (required - the candidate assembly: path or open title; normally the rebuild)

compare_assemblies.output: one line: verified_criteria=PASS/FAIL | components/set | transforms max deltas | mates | dV/dA

save_body_as_part.description: Extract ONE solid body of the ACTIVE (multibody) part into its own .SLDPRT (CreateFeatureFromBody3 on a fresh part from the default template; geometry stays in the SOURCE coordinates; the new doc is saved and closed, the source re-activated). The missing-parts half of flattened-assembly reconstruction (ADR-048): a structured assembly STEP with no per-part files flattens to a multibody import - each DISTINCT body becomes a referenced part file; instances then get transforms via fingerprint match + recovery.

input.state_version: integer (required, must match current execution state)

params.file_path: string (required - target .sldprt path; directory is created)

save_body_as_part.output: -> see execution-response.json - Features = [file name, 'body_name=<source body name>' when available]
